rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EMILIA DI FRANCO GLESSI TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Petitioners Depositions, Compel Production Of Document, And Request For Sanctions.
rulings.Rulings: The motion to compel petitioners' depositions is DENIED without prejudice. The parties are ordered to meet and confer within a week of this order. If the parties cannot reach an agreement regarding the location of the deposition (either in Oakland with reimbursement) or in Washington, respondent reserves the right to move for an order requiring the depositions to take place beyond the statutory geographical limits. The motion to compel production of the letter from Mr. Zweig is DENIED because the letter is a privileged attorney-client communication. The prevailing party shall prepare the proposed order. Any party who wishes to contest this tentative ruling must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than 4:00 p.m. on 9/30/2014.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EMILIA DI FRANCO GLESSI TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Quashing The Notices Of Deposition And Staying Petitioners Anthony Di Franco And Michael Di Francos Depositions, Or Alternatively, For A Protective Order; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Declarations Of Valerie F. Horn, Anthony Di Franco And Michael Di Franco; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Malcolm Leader Picone Esq. And Anthony Muschi In The A
rulings.Rulings: The motion to quash the notices of deposition of Anthony Di Franco and Michael Di Franco is DENIED. The parties must meet and confer within one week of this order to determine whether the deposition should take place in Oakland at the expense of the Respondents or take place in Washington within the mileage parameters provided by C.C.P. Section 2025.250(a). The requests for sanctions are DENIED. The prevailing party shall prepare the proposed order. Should either side wish to contest this tentative ruling, the party must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than 4:00 p.m. on 9/30/2014.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EMILIA DI FRANCO GLESSI TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.
rulings.Rulings: Anthony Muschi's motion for judgment on the pleadings is GRANTED with leave to amend. The Third Amended Petition must be filed within 30 days of this order. The Court takes no position as to whether the currently proposed amended pleading cures the defects of the Second Amended Petition and will only address the pleading that is actually filed. The petitioners do not have standing to vacate Muschi as a beneficiary to the Emilia Di Franco Glessi 2012 Revocable Living Trust. Petitioners have not presented any evidence that they would be entitled to property from the trust if Muschi were disqualified as a beneficiary. The prevailing party shall prepare the proposed order. Should the parties wish to contest the tentative ruling, they should notify the Court no later than 4:00 p.m. on 9/30/2014.
